solutions.description: Add godot prelude import at the top of the file
Add GodotClass derive and base field to the struct

patterns.root_cause: base: Base<T> field is missing from the GodotClass struct

solutions.description: Add base field to the struct

patterns.root_cause: Rust std HashMap used at FFI boundary where Godot Dictionary required
Gd::bind() or Gd::bind_mut() called while already borrowed (re-entrant access)

solutions.description: Extract data before the operation that causes re-entrant access

patterns.root_cause: Godot API called from a spawned thread — Godot is single-threaded

solutions.description: Move Godot API calls to main thread. Use channels to send data back.
solutions.code_change: // Send result via channel, process in _process() on main thread

patterns.root_cause: .gdextension file path wrong or Rust library not built yet

solutions.description: Check .gdextension file library paths match actual build output location

patterns.root_cause: API version mismatch or method hash changed between Godot versions

solutions.description: Ensure gdext version matches your Godot version via api-4-x feature flag

patterns.root_cause: GDExtension .gdextension file not found or library binary missing

solutions.description: Run cargo build to produce the library, then check .gdextension paths

patterns.match: Script inherits from native type.*but is not registered
patterns.root_cause: GodotClass struct not registered in ExtensionLibrary init function

solutions.description: Register the class in your ExtensionLibrary impl

patterns.root_cause: Type mismatch — function expects one type but received another

solutions.description: Check the function signature and ensure argument types match
solutions.code_change:
// Use .into() for compatible type conversions
my_function(value.into())

patterns.root_cause: Value used after being moved into another binding or function call

solutions.description: Clone the value before moving, or restructure to avoid the move

patterns.match: error\[E0502\].*cannot borrow.*as mutable because it is also borrowed as immutable
patterns.root_cause: Simultaneous mutable and immutable borrows of the same value

solutions.description: Restructure code to ensure borrows don't overlap. Extract data to a local variable first.
